threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 bus is just a convenient ride instead of a rolling social experiment that tests your patience daily.
Caught a late 77 last weekend; a guy was reciting Shakespeare to a silent bus full of drunks. Only in Brixton, right? Perfectly bizarre.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy busking with a saxophone while his dogs playfully tugged at a skateboard in the Görlitzer Park sun. That vibe is what I love.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can hear techno music blaring from a bus stop in Friedrichshain at 3 AM, and nobody bats an eye. It's just another night in Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they know to avoid BART on Fridays; the broken brakes and delays are practically a tradition now.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 next to a guy with a surfboard, debating whether to hit Ocean Beach or just head home. Classic SF vibes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume all locals flock to Park Güell, but real Gràcians prefer the quiet charm of Plaça de la Vila. Less photo ops, more everyday life.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still gaping at the street performers, trying to figure out the metro system without Google Maps open.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look lost when trying to navigate the Overground. Everyone's avoiding eye contact, but you’re asking for directions.
Saw a bloke serenading a pigeon on the 59 last night. The bird seemed way more interested than the rest of us. Classic Brixton vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was part of Neukölln? Hearing a group of kids debate the best döner spots while waiting for the bus, completely animated and loud.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you see graffiti artists battling it out in live painting contests while a crowd gathers, sipping on €3 cocktails from the nearest bar.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their reaction to the BART delays—rolling their eyes and finishing their coffee instead of panicking.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today stuck in traffic, and the driver was singing Whitney Houston softly to calm frazzled riders. Only in SF do we get a live concert with our delays.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the stunning plazas, but they miss the hidden cafes with homemade croissants that locals guard fiercely.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look baffled at the metro map—while locals effortlessly swipe their T-10s and glide through the turnstiles.
